compiled_state.title: Orchestrator overrides review classifier tier decision instead of following it
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
The orchestrator (Claude) ignored the review-complexity-classifier.sh output and decided to run a different review tier than what the classifier selected.

Observed behavior: Classifier returned REVIEW_TIER=deep SIZE_ACTION=upgrade. Instead of dispatching the deep tier (3 parallel sonnet agents + opus arch synthesis), the orchestrator rationalized: 'Deep tier with upgrade — that is a lot of review overhead for a commit-and-merge request. Let me dispatch the standard review agent (the classifier flagged upgrade due to diff size, but these are well-tested parallel changes).'

The orchestrator then dispatched a general-purpose sonnet agent instead of any named review agent.

Expected behavior: The orchestrator must follow the classifier output exactly. CLAUDE.md rule: 'You MUST run this command and use its output. Do NOT select a tier based on your assessment of diff complexity or file types — the classifier computes the tier deterministically from the diff.'

Root cause: The orchestrator used its own judgment to override the classifier, which is explicitly prohibited by REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md Step 3.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
